items.title: Gulf Football Federation Approves Duhok to Host Gulf Super Cup
items.desc: Following an official inspection, the Gulf Football Federation endorsed Duhok Stadium's readiness to host the Gulf Super Cup, highlighting the Kurdistan Region's growing role in regional football.

items.title: Zendaya's Ancient Kurdish Jewelry Sparks Global Heritage Debate
items.desc: The appearance of gold plaques attributed to the Zêwye hoard in celebrity jewelry has reignited discussion over Kurdish cultural heritage, archaeological provenance, museum stewardship, and the ethics of incorporating ancient artifacts into luxury fashion.

items.title: Prime Minister Barzani Receives EU Office Head in Erbil as Diplomatic Mission Concludes
items.desc: Kurdistan premier commends Rado Butum's role in strengthening ties between the Kurdistan Region and the European Union

items.title: UK Reaffirms Support for Kurdistan Region During Consul General's Farewell Meeting
items.desc: Prime Minister Masrour Barzani and UK Consul General Andrew Bizley reaffirmed the strength of UK-Kurdistan relations while emphasizing continued cooperation, institutional development, and governance priorities.

items.title: KRG Water Project Poised to Transform Life in 18 Villages of Khalifan District
items.desc: A major drinking water project in Khalifan District is nearing completion, bringing reliable water to 18 villages while supporting the Kurdistan Region's broader strategy to strengthen rural infrastructure and long-term water security.

items.title: Hormuz tensions push India to cancel Iraqi oil cargoes
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad State-owned Indian Oil Corp. on Tuesday canceled plans to load two million barrels of Iraqi crude from the Basra terminal onto the crude carrier (VLCC) Lila Jamnagar around July 23 because of security concerns in the Strait of Hormuz. Quoting three sources, Reuters said another state-run refiner, Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Ltd. (MRPL), also deferred loading an Iraqi

items.title: Baghdad security raid nets three suspects, 25 drone frames
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s National Security Service (INSS) arrested three suspects and seized 25 drone frames from a manufacturing workshop in Baghdad, the agency said on Tuesday. Officers also confiscated carbon-fiber panels, manufactured parts, assembly equipment, and other production materials. The INSS stated that the operation halted production before completion and that investigators

items.title: President Barzani hails French consul’s role in KRI ties
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil Kurdistan Region of Iraq (KRI) President Nechirvan Barzani on Tuesday praised outgoing French Consul Yann Braem for his role in advancing relations between Paris and Erbil during a farewell meeting marking the end of the diplomat’s mission. items.title: Electricity Ministry replaces Iraq’s Sadr City chief
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Electricity Ministry on Tuesday replaced the head of the Sadr City Electricity Distribution Branch in Baghdad and assigned a successor, according to an official document. The ministry relieved Yas Khudair Farhoud of his duties and revoked his managerial allowances after determining that he did not meet the position's job description requirements. It named Mousa Sultan H

items.title: Nineveh readies 100K+ new homes under Iraq housing drive
items.desc: Shafaq News- Nineveh Iraq’s Nineveh province has prepared more than 5,000 hectares for developments planned to provide over 100,000 housing units under Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi’s nationwide residential-plot initiative, Governor Abdul-Qadir Al-Dakhil revealed on Tuesday. Al-Dakhil told a press conference that the sites would be serviced and allocated to different social groups, prior

items.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
items.desc: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
items.desc: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.desc: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

items.title: Tropical Storm Bertha threatens US Gulf coast
items.desc: Storm surges up to 4ft are possible along parts of the Louisiana and Mississippi coasts, officials say.

items.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
items.desc: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.desc: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.desc: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
items.desc: The Houthis, a Shiite militia in Yemen, which is aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
items.desc: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

items.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
items.desc: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.desc: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

items.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
items.desc: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

items.title: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
items.desc: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.
